rawIntent:
[Feature: Build the leave management module — Phase 2: Create Employee domain model and repository]

Create src/modules/employee/employee.model.ts with Employee interface (id, firstName, lastName, email, managerId, role, department). Create src/modules/employee/employee.repository.ts with IEmployeeRepository interface (findById, findByManagerId). No dependencies.

Phase architecture notes:
Employee entity needed for leave requests and approvals.

Detailed phase architecture (architecture-agent):
{"interfaces":["File: src/modules/employee/employee.model.ts\nexport interface Employee {\n  id: string;\n  firstName: string;\n  lastName: string;\n  email: string;\n  managerId: string | null;\n  role: string;\n  department: string | null;\n  createdAt: Date;\n  updatedAt: Date;\n}\n\nexport interface CreateEmployeeDto {\n  firstName: string;\n  lastName: string;\n  email: string;\n  managerId?: string | null;\n  role?: string;\n  department?: string | null;\n}","File: src/modules/employee/employee.repository.ts\nimport { Employee, CreateEmployeeDto } from \"./employee.model\";\n\nexport interface IEmployeeRepository {\n  findById(id: string): Promise<Employee | null>;\n  findByEmail(email: string): Promise<Employee | null>;\n  findByManagerId(managerId: string): Promise<Employee[]>;\n  create(dto: CreateEmployeeDto): Promise<Employee>;\n  update(id: string, dto: Partial<CreateEmployeeDto>): Promise<Employee>;\n}"],"importStatements":["import { Employee, CreateEmployeeDto } from \"./employee.model\";"],"successCriteria":["src/modules/employee/employee.model.ts exists and exports Employee and CreateEmployeeDto","src/modules/employee/employee.repository.ts exists and exports IEmployeeRepository"]}

successCriteria.description: src/modules/employee/employee.model.ts exists and exports Employee and CreateEmployeeDto interfaces with the exact fields specified in the canonical entity fields.
src/modules/employee/employee.repository.ts exists and exports IEmployeeRepository interface with methods findById, findByEmail, findByManagerId, create, and update.

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces (IEmployeeRepository).
No reverse dependencies: The employee module must not depend on leave, policy, balance, or notification modules.
Employee entity fields must exactly match the canonical list: id, firstName, lastName, email, managerId, role, department, createdAt, updatedAt.
CreateEmployeeDto fields must exactly match the canonical list: firstName, lastName, email, managerId, role, department.
outOfScope: Implementing the concrete repository class (e.g., PostgresEmployeeRepository).
Database schema, migrations, or any persistence logic.
Input validation, RBAC enforcement, audit logging, or error handling (deferred to later phases).
Any other modules (leave, policy, balance, notification) or their models/repositories.
All phases beyond Phase 2 as listed in the deferred list.
